ddls:
CREATE TABLE "sizes" (
  "size_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each size */,
  "size_label" TEXT /* Label of the size (e.g., S, M, L, XL) */,
  "bust" REAL /* Bust measurement in inches */,
  "waist" REAL /* Waist measurement in inches */,
  "hips" REAL /* Hips measurement in inches */,
  "dress_length" REAL /* Length of the dress in inches */,
  "sleeve_length" REAL /* Length of the sleeve in inches */,
  "shoulder_width" REAL /* Shoulder width measurement in inches */,
  "chest_depth" REAL /* Chest depth measurement in inches */,
  "inseam_length" REAL /* Inseam length measurement in inches (for bottoms) */,
  "fabric_type" TEXT /* Type of fabric (e.g., cotton, polyester) */,
  "fit_type" TEXT /* Fit type (e.g., regular, slim, relaxed) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("size_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "products" (
  "product_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each product */,
  "product_name" TEXT /* Name of the product */,
  "category_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the category of the product */,
  "size_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the size of the product */,
  "price" REAL /* Price of the product */,
  "stock_quantity" INTEGER /* Quantity of the product in stock */,
  "style_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the style of the product */,
  "material_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the material of the product */,
  "color" TEXT /* Color of the product */,
  "gender" TEXT /* Gender (e.g., Men, Women, Unisex) */,
  "age_group" TEXT /* Age group (e.g., Kids, Adults) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("product_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_products_category_id FOREIGN KEY ("category_id") REFERENCES categories ("category_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_products_size_id FOREIGN KEY ("size_id") REFERENCES sizes ("size_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "categories" (
  "category_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each category */,
  "category_name" TEXT /* Name of the category (e.g., Dresses, Tops) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the category */,
  "parent_category_id" INTEGER /* Reference to a parent category (for hierarchical categorization) */,
  "category_type" TEXT /* Type of category (e.g., Seasonal, Everyday, Special) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("category_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_categories_parent_category_id FOREIGN KEY ("parent_category_id") REFERENCES categories ("category_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "size_standards" (
  "standard_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each size standard */,
  "standard_name" TEXT /* Name of the size standard (e.g., US, UK) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the size standard */,
  "country" TEXT /* Country where the size standard is applicable */,
  "age_range" TEXT /* Age range for which the size standard is intended (e.g., 0-2, 3-6, Adult) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("standard_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "product_sizes" (
  "product_size_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each mapping */,
  "product_id" INTEGER /* ID of the product */,
  "size_id" INTEGER /* ID of the size */,
  "standard_id" INTEGER /* ID of the size standard */,
  "is_default" INTEGER /* Boolean indicating if this is the default size for the product */,
  "available_stock" INTEGER /* Current available stock for this size */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("product_size_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_product_sizes_size_id FOREIGN KEY ("size_id") REFERENCES sizes ("size_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_product_sizes_standard_id FOREIGN KEY ("standard_id") REFERENCES size_standards ("standard_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_product_sizes_product_id FOREIGN KEY ("product_id") REFERENCES products ("product_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "inventory_logs" (
  "log_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each log entry */,
  "product_id" INTEGER /* ID of the product */,
  "change_quantity" INTEGER /* Quantity of change (positive or negative) */,
  "change_date" TEXT /* Date of the change */,
  "reason" TEXT /* Reason for the change (e.g., sale, restock) */,
  "location_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the location where the inventory change occurred */,
  "employee_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the employee who made the change */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("log_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_inventory_logs_product_id FOREIGN KEY ("product_id") REFERENCES products ("product_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "customers" (
  "customer_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each customer */,
  "customer_name" TEXT /* Full name of the customer */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the customer */,
  "phone" TEXT /* Phone number of the customer */,
  "join_date" TEXT /* Date the customer joined */,
  "address" TEXT /* Address of the customer */,
  "city" TEXT /* City of the customer */,
  "state" TEXT /* State of the customer */,
  "zip_code" TEXT /* Zip code of the customer */,
  "country" TEXT /* Country of the customer */,
  "last_purchase_date" TEXT /* Date of the last purchase */,
  "total_spent" REAL /* Total amount spent by the customer */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("customer_id")
)
